title: clinopodium × cadevallii
intro: Clinopodium × cadevallii, also known as Cadevall's Clinopodium, is a hybrid plant that belongs to the mint family and is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which contains camphor, borneol, and bornyl acetate, and is known for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Clinopodium × cadevallii include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory problems, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as an infusion, tincture, or essential oil, and can be applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
